title: Mean-centering is additive for finite signals

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Mean-centering a pointwise sum equals the pointwise sum of the mean-centered signals.
assumptions: u and v are real-valued signals

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: centeredSignal
declarations.statement: def centeredSignal {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - finiteMean s u

declarations.name: centeredSignal_add
declarations.statement: theorem centeredSignal_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) (i : ι) : centeredSignal s (fun j => u j + v j) i = centeredSignal s u i + centeredSignal s v i

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index spectral/DC identity `centeredSignal_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem centeredSignal_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) (i : ι) : centeredSignal s (fun j => u j + v j) i = centeredSignal s u i + centeredSignal s v i
Unfold centeredSignal, use finiteMean_add, and ring-normalize.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or mean-centering lemma needed for the proof.
